questionHtml:
Manny and Annabelle, mathematics teachers at Burnham High School, give their students the same examination. A random sample of the examination scores were collected from each of their classes.

Annabelle uses these scores to conduct a two-tailed t-test to compare the means of the two classes, at the 5% level of significance. It is assumed the examination scores for both classes have the same variance and are normally distributed.

The null hypothesis is μ1=μ2, where μ1 is the mean examination score from Manny’s class and μ2 is the mean examination score from Annabelle’s class.

Annabelle concludes there is insufficient evidence to reject the null hypothesis.

State whether Annabelle’s conclusion is correct. Give a reason for your answer.
markschemeHtml:
0.97652>0.05   0.977>0.05                          R1

Annabelle’s conclusion is correct.                          A1


Note: Do not award R0A1. Answer must reference Annabelle’s conclusion; do not accept an answer, without context, of “fail to reject H0” for the A1 mark.

 

[2 marks]

meta.title: State whether Annabelle’s conclusion is correct. Give a reason for your answer.
breadcrumbLabels: SL 4.11—Expected, observed, hypotheses, chi squared, gof, t-test
